title: Hotel Manager

rationales.taskAutomation: Revenue management, reporting, and pricing are increasingly software-driven, but staff leadership, guest-relations judgment, and property operations stay human-gated.
rationales.toolMaturity: AI-driven revenue-management and dynamic-pricing platforms are mature and standard in mid-size and large hotel operations.
rationales.adoption: Revenue-management software adoption is common across hotel chains; broader operational AI adoption is still spreading.
rationales.agenticExposure: Pricing optimization, occupancy forecasting, and reporting are agent-ownable; staff management and on-property crisis handling are not.

tasksGrowing: Guest-relations judgment for VIP and crisis situations
salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 11-9081, p25-p75 rounded

defenseSkills.skill: AI revenue-management and dynamic-pricing platform fluency

insight: Dynamic pricing algorithms now set most room rates automatically. What a hotel manager still owns is running the property when something goes wrong, staffing gaps, VIP escalations, brand-standard enforcement, none of which the pricing engine touches.
